title: Chapter 8 — Dataset Documentation and Version Control
objectives: Explain why dataset documentation and version control enable reproducibility and collaboration
Assemble metadata, data dictionaries, codebooks, provenance, and annotation notes
Apply documentation practices aligned with FAIR principles and structured formats
Compare Git, DVC, Quilt, DataHub, and Git LFS for dataset versioning
Automate dataset updates and pipeline stages with CI and DVC workflows
Follow best practices for commits, naming, collaboration, and access control
Recognize documentation and versioning patterns in research, industry, and clinical case studies
Anticipate advanced automation, lineage, privacy, and emerging versioning trends

parts.title: Introduction to documentation and version control
parts.bridge_in: Chapters 5 through 7 made datasets cleaner, better understood, and fairer to evaluate. This chapter asks how those assets stay usable over time. This opening part defines dataset documentation and version control, and shows why undocumented or untracked data breaks reproducibility, collaboration, and compliance.

parts.title: Metadata and data dictionaries
parts.bridge_in: Knowing that documentation matters is not enough—teams need concrete components. This part covers metadata fields and data-dictionary entries that describe what each variable means, including units, types, and allowed values.
parts.split_reason: Section 8.2 is split: metadata and dictionaries here; codebooks, provenance, and notes follow in part-03.

parts.title: Codebooks, provenance, and notes
parts.bridge_in: Dictionaries name variables; reuse also needs coded categories, source trails, and caveats. This part covers codebooks that map codes to meanings, provenance that tracks origins and transforms, and annotations that capture sampling limits and sensor faults.
parts.split_reason: Continuation of sec:8.2 after metadata and data dictionaries.

parts.title: Creating effective documentation
parts.bridge_in: With the documentation components named, the next question is how to write them well. This part walks documentation depth by intended use, Markdown and structured formats, and FAIR principles that make datasets findable, accessible, interoperable, and reusable.

parts.title: Version control basics
parts.bridge_in: Documentation freezes meaning at a point in time; version control tracks how the data itself changes. This part defines dataset version control, contrasts traditional and automated approaches, and introduces Git-based commits alongside DVC for large remote-backed assets.

parts.title: Version control tools
parts.bridge_in: Git and DVC are not the only options. This part compares Quilt and DataHub for package-style and streaming datasets, then shows when Git LFS is the right layer for large training artifacts.

parts.title: DVC pipelines and automation
parts.bridge_in: Tool choice becomes operational when pipelines reproduce training from pinned data. This part walks DVC in an ML pipeline, pipeline stage YAML, and live automation patterns including continuous-integration updates.
parts.split_reason: Continuation of sec:8.4 focusing on DVC workflows and automation.

parts.title: Best practices
parts.bridge_in: Pipelines fail when day-to-day habits are weak. This part turns documentation and versioning into operating practice: clear codebooks, provenance notes, descriptive commits, naming conventions, and realistic handling of large binaries, conflicts, and access control.

parts.title: Case studies
parts.bridge_in: Abstract practices become concrete in domain settings. This part shows documentation and versioning at work in climate-agriculture research, predictive maintenance, and clinical-trial collaboration.

parts.title: Advanced topics and emerging trends
parts.bridge_in: The chapter closes by looking beyond static README files and manual commits. This final part covers auto-generated metadata, lineage and privacy-aware storage, plus emerging ideas such as auditability mechanisms and edge-sensor version sync.
parts.split_reason: Merges sec:8.7 advanced topics with sec:8.8 emerging trends for a single closing part.
